examples.title: Basic Daemon Creation
examples.description: Complete C program demonstrating daemon process creation with proper steps
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates all essential steps: fork, setsid, double fork, chdir, close file descriptors, and umask setting. The daemon runs in an infinite loop performing its background tasks.

examples.title: Daemon with PID File and Signal Handling
examples.description: Daemon with PID file management and graceful shutdown handling
examples.explanation: This enhanced daemon includes: PID file creation for process management, signal handlers for graceful SIGTERM/SIGINT shutdown, proper logging to file, and cleanup on exit.

examples.title: Daemon Management Commands
examples.description: Common Linux commands for managing daemon processes
examples.code:
# Check if a process is running (using PID file)
if [ -f /var/run/httpd.pid ]; then
 PID=$(cat /var/run/httpd.pid)
 if kill -0 $PID 2>/dev/null; then
 echo "Process $PID is running"
 fi
fi

# Stop a daemon gracefully (send SIGTERM)
kill $(cat /var/run/mydaemon.pid)

# Force stop a daemon (send SIGKILL)
kill -9 $(cat /var/run/mydaemon.pid)

# List all running processes including daemons
ps -ef | grep -v grep

# List daemon processes (long running)
ps -eo pid,ppid,cmd,etime | grep -E '^\s+1' | head -20

# Using systemctl (systemd based systems)
systemctl start daemon-name
systemctl stop daemon-name
systemctl status daemon-name
systemctl restart daemon-name
systemctl enable daemon-name
examples.explanation: These commands demonstrate how to manage daemon processes: checking status, graceful termination, force kill, listing processes, and using systemd for service management.
